groups.mission: TICKET-002: Thai Knowledge Routing Audit — produce test corpus, harness, and hardening proposals

members.task: You are a senior Thai NLP engineer. Produce a JSON array of exactly 24 Thai-language test prompts for auditing a deterministic routing system that assigns prompts to lanes: innova (code/dev work), chamu (testing/QA), rupa (UI/design), pada (deploy/infra), netra (monitoring/observation). Each element: {"id": "T01".."T24", "prompt": "<realistic Thai prompt>", "expected_lane": "<lane>", "rationale": "<1 sentence Thai>"}. Cover: 8 coding requests, 4 testing, 4 design, 4 deploy, 4 monitoring. Mix formal/casual Thai. Output ONLY the JSON array, no markdown fences.

You are a systems architect reviewing a multi-backend LLM routing system. The system routes jobs deterministically using a Thai syllable splitter hash on routing keys, across lanes with weights (ollama_mdes, thaillm, commandcode weight=18, concurrency limit 2). Known constraints: routing key must be non-empty (past bug), same goal+worker must always route to same backend, circuit breaker trips after 3 consecutive errors with 60s cooldown. Write a determinism hardening proposal in Thai with sections: (1) 5 failure modes that break determinism, (2) concrete mitigation per mode, (3) 3 invariant checks to add to CI. Be specific and implementation-ready. Output markdown.

Write a complete Node.js test file named thai-routing-audit.test.js (no external deps, plain node:assert + node:test). It must: (1) load a prompt corpus from eval/thai-routing-prompts.json (array of {id,prompt,expected_lane}), (2) for each prompt compute a routing lane via a function routeLane(prompt) imported from ../limbs/route-lane.js (assume it exists, document the expected signature), (3) assert determinism by calling routeLane 5 times per prompt and asserting identical results, (4) assert non-empty routing keys, (5) print a summary table pass/fail. Output ONLY the JavaScript code, no markdown fences.

You are a QA engineer specializing in Thai language edge cases. Produce a JSON array of exactly 12 edge-case prompts for testing a Thai text routing system: code-switching Thai-English (3), Thai numerals and Arabic numerals mixed (2), emoji-heavy (2), transliterated English in Thai script (2), very long prompt 500+ chars (1), whitespace/zero-width chars (1), empty-adjacent like single space (1). Each element: {"id": "E01".."E12", "prompt": "<the edge case text>", "category": "<category>", "expected_behavior": "<what a robust router must do, in Thai>"}. Output ONLY the JSON array, no markdown fences.

You are a security-minded QA lead. Write an adversarial test checklist (markdown, in Thai) for an LLM routing system with file-based message bus at /tmp/manusat-bus/<agent>/. Cover: (1) prompt injection attempting to change routing lane, (2) empty/null routing keys, (3) path traversal in agent names, (4) oversized payloads, (5) unicode normalization attacks, (6) concurrent write races on inbox files. For each: attack scenario, expected safe behavior, one-line test idea. 6 sections, concise, implementation-ready.

groups.mission: TICKET-006 Phase 2: Manus-pattern integration PoC for innomcp — request_id wrapper, skill registration, tests

members.task: Write a complete Node.js module manus-wrapper.js (CommonJS, no external deps) implementing the Manus response pattern for a multi-agent system: (1) generateRequestId() returning 'req_' + timestamp + random suffix, (2) wrapOk(request_id, data) returning {ok:true, request_id, data}, (3) wrapErr(request_id, code, message) returning {ok:false, request_id, error:{code,message}}, (4) withRequestId(fn) higher-order wrapper that injects request_id, catches exceptions into wrapErr, and logs '[req_id] start/end/error' lines, (5) module.exports for all. Include JSDoc on every function. Output ONLY JavaScript, no markdown fences.

Design skill registration definitions following the Manus Skills pattern for 3 organs of a multi-agent system: mouth (broadcast message to agent inbox: inputs agent/message/prefix, outputs message_id/delivered_at/inbox_size), oracle (knowledge search: inputs query/limit, outputs results[]/search_time_ms), verifier (test runner: inputs test_suite/tests[], outputs passed/failed/total/pass_rate/failures[]). Output a single JSON object: {"skills": [ {"id", "name", "description", "inputs": {<field>: {"type", "required", "default?"}}, "outputs": {<field>: {"type"}}, "errors": [{"code", "when"}］} x3 ]}. Use proper JSON (no ］, use ]). Output ONLY JSON, no markdown fences.

Write a complete Node.js test file manus-wrapper.test.js using node:test and node:assert (no external deps) for a module ../limbs/manus-wrapper.js exporting generateRequestId, wrapOk, wrapErr, withRequestId. Tests: (1) request ids unique across 1000 calls and match /^req_/, (2) wrapOk shape exact, (3) wrapErr shape exact, (4) withRequestId passes through return value wrapped in ok envelope, (5) withRequestId converts thrown Error into {ok:false,...} with same request_id, (6) async function support. Output ONLY JavaScript, no markdown fences.

Write a webhook handler specification (markdown, Thai) for task-completion webhooks in a multi-agent system following the Manus pattern. Endpoint: POST /webhooks/manus/:event with body {request_id, task_id, project_id, status, data, timestamp}. Spec sections: (1) signature validation flow, (2) event types table (task:completed, task:failed, input:required) with required actions, (3) error cases table: bad signature 401, unknown event 400, replayed request_id 409, oversized body 413, handler crash 500 — each with response body and retry semantics, (4) idempotency strategy, (5) 5 acceptance criteria. Concise, implementation-ready.

Write an end-to-end integration test plan (markdown, Thai) for: agent task submitted -> skill executes (mouth broadcast writes file to /tmp/manusat-bus/<agent>/) -> webhook fires on completion -> requesting agent receives notification. Sections: (1) test environment setup steps, (2) 6 E2E scenarios (happy path, skill error, webhook endpoint down, duplicate request_id, concurrent 10 tasks, bus directory missing) each with given/when/then, (3) verification commands (bash one-liners), (4) pass criteria. Implementation-ready, concise.
